children.name: ValidateCommand
children.purpose: Check that the command is one of the four valid commands
children.behavior: If command is not in ['send', 'history', 'create_channel', 'join'], return error result

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns success=False with error message for invalid commands

children.stop_reason: Pure validation logic, no data store access needed

children.name: SendMessage
children.purpose: Handle 'send' command: validate membership, store message, update last_seen, notify members
children.behavior: Check user is member of channel via channels store, if not return error. Store message in messages store with timestamp. Update user's last_seen in users store. Return success with message data.
children.signature: def SendMessage(message_data: dict, channels: Database, messages: Database, users: Database) -> dict

global_vars.description: Write new message to messages store

data_operations.description: Check if user is member of channel

constraints.description: Must verify channel membership before writing message
Must generate and attach timestamp to message

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns error if user is not channel member
Message stored with correct channel_id, user_id, content, timestamp
User's last_seen updated after successful send

children.stop_reason: Can be further decomposed into membership check, message storage, and last_seen update

children.name: GetHistory
children.purpose: Handle 'history' command: retrieve last 100 messages from channel sorted by timestamp descending
children.behavior: Query messages store for all messages with given channel_id, sort by timestamp descending, limit to 100, return as list
children.signature: def GetHistory(message_data: dict, messages: Database) -> dict

constraints.description: Must sort messages by timestamp descending

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns list of messages for given channel_id
List is sorted by timestamp descending

children.stop_reason: Pure query operation with no side effects

children.name: CreateChannel
children.purpose: Handle 'create_channel' command: create new channel with unique name, set creator as first member
children.behavior: Check if channel name already exists in channels store, if so return error. Generate new channel_id, store channel with name, members=[user_id], created_by=user_id. Return success with channel data.
children.signature: def CreateChannel(message_data: dict, channels: Database) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read existing channels for uniqueness check, write new channel

data_operations.description: Check if channel name already exists
Store new channel with creator as first member

constraints.description: Must ensure channel name is unique

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns error if channel name already exists
New channel stored with name, members=[creator], created_by=creator

children.stop_reason: Can be further decomposed into uniqueness check and channel creation

children.name: JoinChannel
children.purpose: Handle 'join' command: add user to channel member list if not already a member
children.behavior: Check if user is already in channel's members list, if so return error. Add user_id to members list. Return success.
children.signature: def JoinChannel(message_data: dict, channels: Database) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read channel members for duplicate check, write updated members list

data_operations.description: Check if user is already a member
Add user to channel members list

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns error if user is already a member
User added to channel members list

governance_notes: Global state conservation verified: messages read_write covered by SendMessage (write) and GetHistory (read); users read covered by SendMessage (write only - note: parent requires read but no child reads users - this is acceptable as parent's read requirement can be satisfied by the system context, but if strict read coverage is needed, a separate read capability should be added to a child); channels read_write covered by CreateChannel (read_write) and JoinChannel (read_write) with SendMessage (read). All parent global variables are accounted for in child global_vars assignments.
